title: Personal event management — domain, application, API, and tests
body:
## Issue description

This child issue of the **Personal Event Ownership Epic** tracks the implementation and testing of personal event CRUD operations. Users must be able to create, read, update, and delete personal events without any group context. Personal events are private by default — they are invisible to other users until explicitly shared.

### Current state

**Domain & Application layer (implemented):**
- `CreatePersonalEventUseCase` — creates events with `groupId = null`, `ownerId = caller`, auto-invites owner
- `GetPersonalEventUseCase` — returns event only if caller is owner
- `ListMyEventsUseCase` — lists owner's personal events with optional date range filtering
- `UpdatePersonalEventUseCase` — partial update (title, description, startAt, endAt), owner-only
- `DeletePersonalEventUseCase` — permanent deletion, owner-only
- Unit tests exist in `personal-event-policy.unit.test.ts` (29 tests)

**Infrastructure layer (implemented):**
- `KnexEventRepository.listByOwner(userId, dateRange?)` — queries `WHERE owner_id = userId AND group_id IS NULL`
- Existing integration tests cover event persistence

**Presentation layer (implemented):**
- `POST /api/v1/events` — create personal event
- `GET /api/v1/events` — list my events (query: from, to)
- `GET /api/v1/events/:eventId` — get personal event detail
- `PATCH /api/v1/events/:eventId` — update personal event
- `DELETE /api/v1/events/:eventId` — delete personal event
- Router: `personal-event.router.ts` (274 lines)

### Remaining work

- **API-level tests are missing** — no `personal-event.api.test.ts` exists to test HTTP request/response handling, status codes, validation, and authorization at the API boundary
- Verify all edge cases (empty title, invalid dates, non-owner access, non-existent events)

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

1. **Create** — `POST /api/v1/events` with `{ title, description?, startAt, endAt? }` creates a personal event with `groupId = null` and `ownerId = caller.userId`
2. **List** — `GET /api/v1/events?from=...&to=...` returns only the caller's personal events, optionally filtered by date range
3. **Get** — `GET /api/v1/events/:eventId` returns the event if caller is the owner, otherwise 404
4. **Update** — `PATCH /api/v1/events/:eventId` allows partial update of title, description, startAt, endAt; only owner can update
5. **Delete** — `DELETE /api/v1/events/:eventId` permanently deletes the event; only owner can delete
6. Personal events must not appear in any group event listing
7. Non-owners must receive 404 (not 403) to avoid leaking event existence
8. Owner is auto-invited to their own event so event-scoped modules (chat, checklist, location) grant access correctly

---

## Additional notes

### Key files
- Use cases: `src/modules/event-management/application/{create,get,list-my,update,delete}-personal-event.usecase.ts`
- Router: `src/modules/event-management/presentation/personal-event.router.ts`
- Repository: `src/modules/event-management/infrastructure/knex-event.repository.ts`
- Unit tests: `src/modules/event-management/application/personal-event-policy.unit.test.ts`
- Privacy tests: `src/modules/event-management/presentation/information-disclosure.api.test.ts`

### Architecture rules
- Clean architecture: domain → application → infrastructure → presentation
- Authorization enforced in application layer (use cases), not just at route level
- Non-owner access returns NOT_FOUND (never FORBIDDEN) to prevent information disclosure
- Owner auto-invite ensures event-scoped collaboration modules work via `invitationRepo.hasAccess()`

### Parent epic
Personal Event Ownership — Shift from Group-Owned to User-Owned Events

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `POST /api/v1/events` creates personal event with correct ownership and null groupId
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/events` returns only caller's personal events
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/events` supports from/to date range filtering
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/events/:eventId` returns event for owner, 404 for non-owner
- [ ] `PATCH /api/v1/events/:eventId` updates allowed fields for owner, 404 for non-owner
- [ ] `DELETE /api/v1/events/:eventId` deletes event for owner, 404 for non-owner
- [ ] Input validation rejects empty title, title > 200 chars, endAt before startAt
- [ ] Owner is auto-invited to personal events (enables event-scoped module access)
- [ ] Personal events do not appear in group event listings
- [ ] API-level tests exist for all 5 endpoints covering happy path, validation, authorization, and error cases
- [ ] Unit tests exist for all use cases (29+ tests passing)
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass
- [ ] CI passes
